journey.title: Your Journey
journey.subtitle: Everyone has their own growth path. PuzzleMe helps you see it.
journey.selfDesc: Log fragments, fuse experiences, generate action cards — your growth, all in one view.
journey.coCreateReady1: You have enough fragments! Try Co-Creation!
journey.coCreateReady2: Quite a few fragments. Want to collaborate?
journey.coCreateReady3: Keep collecting. 8+ fragments to unlock Co-Creation.

processValue.title: Process IS Value
processValue.desc: PuzzleMe is not just a tool, it's a process. When you start logging fragments and reflecting on experiences, change is already happening.
processValue.quote: What matters is not what you piece together, but what you discover in the process.

different.title: Why PuzzleMe is Different
different.subtitle: We're not just another chatbot. We're your growth partner.

steps.title: 4 Steps to Start Your Growth Journey
steps.subtitle: Simple 4 steps to begin your self-exploration journey
steps.step1Desc: Record experiences, ideas, and feedback from daily life. AI helps extract structured fragments.
steps.step2Desc: AI fuses your fragments with others' to discover capabilities you didn't see.
steps.step3Desc: Based on fusion results, generate executable action cards for real implementation.
steps.step4Desc: Check-in to record progress. AI continuously tracks. Capability map keeps upgrading.

map.title: Journey Map
map.subtitle: See your growth path. Every step counts.
map.exampleDesc: Teacher Wang used it for 3 months, from fragment logging to capability monetization.
map.progressTip: Every fragment logged lights up a grid on the map.

teacher.title: Real Case: Teacher Wang
teacher.subtitle: See how a real user transformed their career path with PuzzleMe
teacher.persona: "I've been teaching for 10 years, but I always felt my value was more than this."
teacher.description: Teacher Wang, 35, middle school Chinese teacher, wants to develop a side business but doesn't know where to start.
teacher.beforeItem1: Teaching insights written in notebook, haven't looked at it in ages
teacher.beforeItem2: Students say she 'explains well' but didn't think much of it
teacher.beforeItem3: Wants to do knowledge monetization but doesn't know what to teach
teacher.quote: Turns out my most valuable asset isn't Chinese knowledge, it's the ability to explain complex things clearly.
evidence: 10 years of teaching, students evaluate 'easy to understand'
Mentioned 3 times in fragments: 'students say I explain well'
Habit of organizing notes (indicates strong summarization skills)
actions: This week: Record a 3-min video 'How to Understand Ancient Poetry', post on TikTok
Organize 3 'Chinese knowledge points students love most', make into PDF
Post 'Chinese Teacher's Cognitive Awakening' on Knowledge Planet
results: TikTok gained 3000+ followers, first video 120k views
Parents actively consulting 'how to help kids learn Chinese'
teacher.final: Teacher Wang now does: Teaching + TikTok knowledge creator + Knowledge Planet
teacher.footnote: She says: 'If not for PuzzleMe, I'd probably still be writing insights in my notebook.'

rider.title: Case: Delivery Rider Xiao Li
rider.fragmentsDesc: Familiar with nearby restaurants, good at route planning, positive customer reviews
rider.quote: I realized I know this area better than the station manager.
rider.direction: Direction: Local life guide / Start own delivery business
rider.income: Xiao Li now earns ¥12000+ pre-tax, planning his own delivery team

moreCases.next: Product managers, designers, programmers, sales... your industry is on the way

cta.tagline: You have many fragments scattered about you — come to PuzzleMe and piece together a self you didn't know.
